note: Revision 2 focuses on Phase 1 Tasks 1.1 + 1.2 of docs/plans/2026-04-24-dialogue-pipeline-completion.md: dialogue translation_nl population (11 dialogue_chunk items) and dialogue cloze authoring (7 new clozes + 4 clozeSkips). Non-dialogue content (174 vocab clozes, grammar patterns, candidates, vocab-enrichments) is unchanged since revision 1 and carries forward its prior WARNING list — not re-flagged here. Zero CRITICAL regressions introduced by the dialogue work.

issues.item: dialogue cloze for 'tidak begitu sakit dokter, sedikit saja.' blanking `sakit`
issues.issue: In isolation (the way cloze exercises are surfaced at review time), the Indonesian frame 'Tidak begitu ___ dokter, sedikit saja.' is ambiguous: multiple lesson-9 adjectives fit the slot with comparable naturalness — `takut` (bang — 'niet zo bang dokter, maar een beetje'), `pusing` (duizelig), `mual` (misselijk), `lemah` (zwak), even `capèk` (moe). The creator hedged on this one. The Dutch translation_text 'Niet zo erg pijn dokter, maar een beetje.' names 'pijn' directly, which resolves the ambiguity for the learner who reads the translation — so this is not CRITICAL per 13d, but the Indonesian sentence under-tests vocabulary retrieval: a learner who only glances at the Dutch will get it from the gloss rather than from the Indonesian frame. Not blocking; admin can either accept (translation-driven disambiguation is a valid fallback) or rewrite the source_text to add a stronger Indonesian prime (e.g. include a prior-sentence reference: 'Kaki saya tidak begitu ___ dokter, sedikit saja.' would tie the slot to the limb-pain frame).
issues.fix: Optional: strengthen the Indonesian prime by retaining a topical word from the surrounding turn (e.g. 'Kaki saya tidak begitu ___, sedikit saja.'), or accept as-is and rely on the Dutch translation for disambiguation.

issues.issue: In the lesson-9 vocabulary list `kaki` is glossed twice — page 4 'voet' and page 9 'been'. The dialogue translations consistently use 'voet' throughout (e.g. 'Mijn voet doet heel erg pijn, dokter.' / 'laat me eerst je voet onderzoeken' / 'zodat je voet snel beter wordt'). Medically, a child who fell from a tree and says 'Kaki saya sakit sekali' is in normal Dutch usage talking about her leg/been rather than her foot specifically, and the dialogue follow-ups ('jalan hati-hati' = walk carefully, 'jangan lagi naik pohon biar kakimu lekas sembuh' = so the leg heals) read more naturally with 'been'. 'Voet' is lexically correct and consistent with the page-4 gloss, so not CRITICAL — flagging only so admin can decide whether to harmonise on 'been' for the fall-from-tree narrative.
issues.fix: Optional: switch all four 'voet' occurrences in the dialogue translation_nl fields to 'been' to match the more natural Dutch reading for a fall-from-tree injury, or leave as-is (both are valid glosses listed in the vocabulary).

issues.item: 'Tidak begitu sakit dokter, sedikit saja.' translation_nl uses 'maar een beetje'
issues.issue: The Indonesian `sedikit saja` is 'just a little' — an attenuator, not a contrast. The Dutch 'Niet zo erg pijn dokter, maar een beetje.' is grammatically fine and reads naturally in Dutch, but 'maar' introduces a mild contrastive flavour that isn't in the Indonesian. A slightly closer rendering would be 'Niet zo erg pijn, dokter, een beetje maar.' or 'Niet zo erg pijn, dokter, maar weinig.' Minor register nuance; current translation is acceptable.
issues.fix: Optional: 'Niet zo erg pijn, dokter, een beetje maar.' — preserves the attenuative sense of `sedikit saja` without the implied contrast.

checks.lesson_ts_structure: PASS -- unchanged since revision 1; grammar sections use categories[], exercises use sections[]
checks.grammar_patterns_fields: PASS -- unchanged since revision 1; all 3 patterns have required fields
checks.dialogue_translations: PASS -- all 11 dialogue_chunk items have populated translation_nl. Natural Dutch throughout. Register correctly reflects speaker relationships per sections-catalog: doctor-to-child uses familiar 'je/jij' ('Hoe kun je nou uit een boom vallen?', 'Straks ben je vast heel snel weer beter, Tin.'); doctor-to-mother uses formal address ('Echt waar, mevrouw, er is niets aan de hand'); mother-to-doctor uses formal concern ('Echt waar, dokter? ... Ik was zo bang!'); patient-to-doctor preserves 'dokter' as honorific vocative. Idioms handled idiomatically: 'Nah' -> 'Nou', 'coba' -> 'kom', 'ya' -> 'hè', 'Bu' -> 'mevrouw', 'tidak apa-apa' -> 'er is niets aan de hand' / 'niets gebroken'. Proper nouns (Tina, Tin, PUSKESMAS) retained. Two minor nuance WARNINGs flagged but non-blocking.
checks.dialogue_cloze_quality: PASS -- 6 of 7 dialogue clozes are uniquely primed by their Indonesian context (pohon after 'jatuh dari', tinggi after 'lebih ... di pohon', periksa with 'kakimu dulu', sembuh after 'cepat sekali', takut in 'Saya ___ sekali!', hati-hati in 'jalan ___'). The 7th (`sakit` in 'Tidak begitu ___ dokter, sedikit saja.') is semantically ambiguous in isolation but disambiguated by the Dutch translation which contains 'pijn' — flagged as WARNING not CRITICAL per 13d because the translation surfaced pre-answer resolves the ambiguity. All blanked words pass 13c: each exists in current or prior lesson learning-items.ts (pohon lesson-8; tinggi lesson-7; periksa/sembuh/takut/hati-hati lesson-9; sakit lesson-2), and each POS has >=2 same-POS siblings in lesson-9 pool (adjectives: diam/hati-hati/puas/takut/pusing/mual/lemah/capèk; verbs: awas/bekerja/henti/jatuh/kaget/kumpul/loncat/patah/sembuh/minum-obat/muntah/dibawa/periksa; nouns: many). No blanks are grammar particles, pronouns, discourse particles, or proper nouns. All source_text fields preserve original punctuation and contain exactly one ___.
checks.cloze_skips_validity: PASS -- all 4 clozeSkips entries use reason 'below_6_token_threshold' from the allowed set. Token counts verified by whitespace split: 'Ada apa?' = 2 tokens; 'Bagaimana bisa jatuh dari pohon?' = 5 tokens; 'Awas dokter, jangan sakit, ya.' = 5 tokens; 'Ini sakit atau tidak?' = 4 tokens. All <6. No false skips (i.e. no line that qualifies for a cloze got skipped).
checks.dialogue_coverage: PASS -- all 11 dialogue_chunk items in learning-items.ts appear in exactly one of clozeContexts (7) or clozeSkips (4). No double-coverage and no uncovered chunks.
